# Getting Support

Thanks for using CMDS. This guide applies to every repository in the [CMDS organization](https://github.com/cmds-app) unless an individual repo overrides it with its own `SUPPORT.md`.

## Where to ask

Pick the channel that matches your question:

| You want to...                                  | Go here |
| :---------------------------------------------- | :------ |
| Get help as a Keyera CMDS customer or end user  | [Keyera CMDS support](https://www.keyeracmds.com/support/) |
| Report a bug or unexpected behavior             | Open a [Fix](../../issues/new?template=fix.yml) issue on the affected repository |
| Request a new feature or change                 | Open a [Feature](../../issues/new?template=feature.yml) issue on the affected repository |
| Report a security vulnerability                 | Follow [`SECURITY.md`](./SECURITY.md) — **do not open a public issue** |
| Contribute code or documentation                | See [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](./CONTRIBUTING.md) |

## Before opening an issue

1. Check the repository's `README.md` for setup, configuration, and troubleshooting notes.
2. Search [existing issues](../../issues?q=is%3Aissue) — your question may already be answered.
3. Confirm you are running a [supported version](./SECURITY.md#supported-versions).

If your question is not covered by the above, open a new issue using the template that best matches your request. Include enough detail (version, environment, steps to reproduce) for a maintainer to act on it.

## Response expectations

CMDS repositories are maintained on a best-effort basis. Issues are triaged in priority order, with security and production-impacting bugs handled first. There is no guaranteed response time for community-reported issues; paying customers should use the [Keyera CMDS support](https://www.keyeracmds.com/support/) channel for SLA-backed support.
